Now let me create a comprehensive summary report based on my thorough exploration: ## COMPREHENSIVE REACT APP CODEBASE ANALYSIS ### 1. PROVIDER TREE & CONTEXT **Full Provider Tree (Root → Leaf):** ``` createRoot(document.getElementById('root')!) ├── React.StrictMode │ └── LikedArticlesProvider │ └── App ``` **Provider Details:** - **LikedArticlesProvider**: `/src/contexts/LikedArticlesContext.tsx` - Manages liked articles state - Provides context: `{ likedArticles, toggleLike, isLiked }` - Renders heart animation overlay when article is liked - Custom hook: `useLikedArticles()` **External Library Providers:** - **Analytics**: `@vercel/analytics/react` (imported in App.tsx as `` component) --- ### 2. CSS FILES & IMPORTS **All CSS Files with Import Paths:** | File Path | Purpose | |-----------|---------| | `/src/index.css` | Global styles - Tailwind import, scrollbar hiding, body overflow handling | | `/src/assets/heartAnimation.css` | Heart pop animation for likes (imported in LikedArticlesContext) | | `/src/styles/Article.css` | Article container & content styling (imported via component use) | | `/src/stories/button.css` | Storybook button styles | | `/src/stories/header.css` | Storybook header styles | | `/src/stories/page.css` | Storybook page styles | **CSS Framework:** Tailwind CSS v4.0.3 (via `@tailwindcss/postcss` and `@tailwindcss/vite`) --- ### 3. DATA FETCHING QUERIES **All Fetch Calls:** **useWikiArticles Hook** (`/src/hooks/useWikiArticles.ts`): - **Function:** `fetchArticles(forBuffer?: boolean)` - **Method:** Native `fetch()` API - **Base URL Pattern:** `{currentLanguage.api}` (language-dependent Wikipedia endpoint) - **Query Parameters:** ``` action=query format=json generator=random grnnamespace=0 prop=extracts|info|pageimages inprop=url|varianttitles grnlimit=20 exintro=1 exlimit=max exsentences=5 explaintext=1 piprop=thumbnail pithumbsize=800 origin=* variant={currentLanguage.id} ``` - **Response Handling:** Parses Wikipedia query response, filters articles with thumbnails/extracts/URLs - **Image Preloading:** Uses `Promise.allSettled()` for image preload verification - **Buffer System:** Maintains 20-article buffer for smooth infinite scrolling **Wikipedia API Endpoints** (by language, from `/src/languages.ts`): - English: `https://en.wikipedia.org/w/api.php?` - Plus 40+ other language variants (Arabic, Bengali, Catalan, Czech, German, Spanish, French, Japanese, Chinese, etc.) --- ### 4. BROWSER STATE READS **localStorage Usage:** | Key | File | Purpose | |-----|------|---------| | `"lang"` | `/src/hooks/useLocalization.ts` | Persists selected language preference | | `"likedArticles"` | `/src/contexts/LikedArticlesContext.tsx` | Persists user's liked Wikipedia articles | **localStorage Operations:** 1. **Read on Init:** - `localStorage.getItem("lang")` - Line 6 (useLocalization.ts) - `localStorage.getItem("likedArticles")` - Line 16 (LikedArticlesContext.tsx) 2. **Write on Change:** - `localStorage.setItem("lang", currentLanguage.id)` - Line 15 (useLocalization.ts) - `localStorage.setItem("likedArticles", JSON.stringify(likedArticles))` - Line 23 (LikedArticlesContext.tsx) **No sessionStorage or cookies used.** --- ### 5. PORTALS & PORTAL ROOTS **Portal Root Elements:** - **Single Root:** `
` in `/index.html` (Line 15) - **Mount Point:** `createRoot(document.getElementById('root')!)` in `/src/main.tsx` (Line 7) **No React Portals (createPortal) used in this codebase.** All UI rendered as normal DOM hierarchy. --- ### 6. KEY COMPONENTS & PAGES **Component Tree & Props:** ``` App (Main Component) ├── WikiCard[] │ └── Props: { article: WikiArticle } │ └── Features: Image lazy loading, like/share actions, double-click to like ├── LanguageSelector │ └── Props: None (uses context) │ └── Features: Dropdown with 40+ language options ├── Analytics (from Vercel) │ └── Props: None └── Modal Overlays (inline, conditional render) ├── About Modal ├── Liked Articles Modal │ └── ArticleList (used here) │ └── Props: { articles: ArticleProps[], onArticleSelect? } │ └── Renders: Article components │ └── Article Component │ └── Props: { title, content, image? } ``` **Page Structure:** - **Single Page Application** - No routing (all components in App.tsx) - **Main UI:** Infinite-scrolling fullscreen cards (snap scroll) - **Modals:** 1. About modal (fixed overlay, z-index: 50) 2. Liked Articles modal with search functionality --- ### 7. ROUTING SETUP **Routing:** None. This is a **single-page React app** with no react-router or equivalent. Navigation: - Button clicks toggle local state (`showAbout`, `showLikes`) - Language changes trigger `window.location.reload()` - External links open in new tabs (Wikipedia articles) --- ### 8. PROJECT STRUCTURE SUMMARY ``` /src ├── main.tsx # Entry point, wraps App in providers ├── App.tsx # Main component (infinite scroll UI) ├── index.css # Global Tailwind styles ├── languages.ts # Language definitions (40+ Wikipedia languages) ├── vite-env.d.ts # Vite type declarations ├── contexts/ │ └── LikedArticlesContext.tsx # Like management + heart animation ├── hooks/ │ ├── useWikiArticles.ts # Wikipedia API fetching + buffering │ └── useLocalization.ts # Language persistence ├── components/ │ ├── WikiCard.tsx # Main card component (fullscreen card) │ ├── Article.tsx # Article display (semantic HTML) │ ├── ArticleList.tsx # Article list renderer │ └── LanguageSelector.tsx # Language dropdown ├── types/ │ └── ArticleProps.ts # ArticleProps interface ├── styles/ │ └── Article.css # Article container styles ├── assets/ │ └── heartAnimation.css # Heart animation keyframes └── stories/ # Storybook test stories ├── Button.tsx/stories.ts ├── Header.tsx/stories.ts ├── Page.tsx/stories.ts └── *.css files ``` --- ### 9. DEPENDENCIES SUMMARY **Core:** - React 18.3.1 + React-DOM 18.3.1 - Tailwind CSS 4.0.3 - Lucide React 0.474.0 (icons) - Vercel Analytics 1.4.1 **Build Tools:** - Vite 6.0.5 - TypeScript ~5.6.2 - Storybook (pre-release build) - Vitest + Playwright (testing) --- ### 10. REAL USAGE PATTERNS **Infinite Scroll Pattern:** - Uses Intersection Observer to detect when user scrolls near bottom - Triggers `fetchArticles()` with 100px margin - Articles buffered (20 pre-fetched) for smooth UX - Loading indicator shown during fetch **Like/Favorites Pattern:** - Double-click or heart button to like - Displays heart pop animation (0.5s ease-out from center-top) - Persists to localStorage immediately - Exportable as JSON with timestamp **Language Switching Pattern:** - Select language from dropdown - Saves to localStorage - Full page reload with new language variant - All API calls use `variant` parameter for correct title display **Share Pattern:** - Uses native Navigator.share() if available (mobile) - Falls back to clipboard copy on desktop - Alert confirmation on fallback --- ### 11. ANALYTICS & TRACKING - **Vercel Analytics** component rendered in App (no custom tracking) - No event tracking setup observed beyond page views --- All files have been thoroughly read and analyzed. The app is a TikTok-style Wikipedia browser ("WikiTok") with minimal complexity, no routing, single provider (likes context), and direct Wikipedia API integration across 40+ languages.

PROVIDER TREE & CONTEXT **Full Provider Tree (Root → Leaf):** ``` createRoot(document.getElementById('root')!) ├── React.StrictMode │ └── LikedArticlesProvider │ └── App ``` **Provider Details:** - **LikedArticlesProvider**: `/src/contexts/LikedArticlesContext.tsx` - Manages liked articles state - Provides context: `{ likedArticles, toggleLike, isLiked }` - Renders heart animation overlay when article is liked - Custom hook: `useLikedArticles()` **External Library Providers:** - **Analytics**: `@vercel/analytics/react` (imported in App.tsx as `` component) --- ### 2. CSS FILES & IMPORTS **All CSS Files with Import Paths:** | File Path | Purpose | |-----------|---------| | `/src/index.css` | Global styles - Tailwind import, scrollbar hiding, body overflow handling | | `/src/assets/heartAnimation.css` | Heart pop animation for likes (imported in LikedArticlesContext) | | `/src/styles/Article.css` | Article container & content styling (imported via component use) | | `/src/stories/button.css` | Storybook button styles | | `/src/stories/header.css` | Storybook header styles | | `/src/stories/page.css` | Storybook page styles | **CSS Framework:** Tailwind CSS v4.0.3 (via `@tailwindcss/postcss` and `@tailwindcss/vite`) --- ### 3. DATA FETCHING QUERIES **All Fetch Calls:** **useWikiArticles Hook** (`/src/hooks/useWikiArticles.ts`): - **Function:** `fetchArticles(forBuffer?: boolean)` - **Method:** Native `fetch()` API - **Base URL Pattern:** `{currentLanguage.api}` (language-dependent Wikipedia endpoint) - **Query Parameters:** ``` action=query format=json generator=random grnnamespace=0 prop=extracts|info|pageimages inprop=url|varianttitles grnlimit=20 exintro=1 exlimit=max exsentences=5 explaintext=1 piprop=thumbnail pithumbsize=800 origin=* variant={currentLanguage.id} ``` - **Response Handling:** Parses Wikipedia query response, filters articles with thumbnails/extracts/URLs - **Image Preloading:** Uses `Promise.allSettled()` for image preload verification - **Buffer System:** Maintains 20-article buffer for smooth infinite scrolling **Wikipedia API Endpoints** (by language, from `/src/languages.ts`): - English: `https://en.wikipedia.org/w/api.php?` - Plus 40+ other language variants (Arabic, Bengali, Catalan, Czech, German, Spanish, French, Japanese, Chinese, etc.) --- ### 4. BROWSER STATE READS **localStorage Usage:** | Key | File | Purpose | |-----|------|---------| | `"lang"` | `/src/hooks/useLocalization.ts` | Persists selected language preference | | `"likedArticles"` | `/src/contexts/LikedArticlesContext.tsx` | Persists user's liked Wikipedia articles | **localStorage Operations:** 1. **Read on Init:** - `localStorage.getItem("lang")` - Line 6 (useLocalization.ts) - `localStorage.getItem("likedArticles")` - Line 16 (LikedArticlesContext.tsx) 2. **Write on Change:** - `localStorage.setItem("lang", currentLanguage.id)` - Line 15 (useLocalization.ts) - `localStorage.setItem("likedArticles", JSON.stringify(likedArticles))` - Line 23 (LikedArticlesContext.tsx) **No sessionStorage or cookies used.** --- ### 5. PORTALS & PORTAL ROOTS **Portal Root Elements:** - **Single Root:** `
` in `/index.html` (Line 15) - **Mount Point:** `createRoot(document.getElementById('root')!)` in `/src/main.tsx` (Line 7) **No React Portals (createPortal) used in this codebase.** All UI rendered as normal DOM hierarchy. --- ### 6. KEY COMPONENTS & PAGES **Component Tree & Props:** ``` App (Main Component) ├── WikiCard[] │ └── Props: { article: WikiArticle } │ └── Features: Image lazy loading, like/share actions, double-click to like ├── LanguageSelector │ └── Props: None (uses context) │ └── Features: Dropdown with 40+ language options ├── Analytics (from Vercel) │ └── Props: None └── Modal Overlays (inline, conditional render) ├── About Modal ├── Liked Articles Modal │ └── ArticleList (used here) │ └── Props: { articles: ArticleProps[], onArticleSelect? } │ └── Renders: Article components │ └── Article Component │ └── Props: { title, content, image? } ``` **Page Structure:** - **Single Page Application** - No routing (all components in App.tsx) - **Main UI:** Infinite-scrolling fullscreen cards (snap scroll) - **Modals:** 1. About modal (fixed overlay, z-index: 50) 2. Liked Articles modal with search functionality --- ### 7. ROUTING SETUP **Routing:** None. This is a **single-page React app** with no react-router or equivalent. Navigation: - Button clicks toggle local state (`showAbout`, `showLikes`) - Language changes trigger `window.location.reload()` - External links open in new tabs (Wikipedia articles) --- ### 8. PROJECT STRUCTURE SUMMARY ``` /src ├── main.tsx # Entry point, wraps App in providers ├── App.tsx # Main component (infinite scroll UI) ├── index.css # Global Tailwind styles ├── languages.ts # Language definitions (40+ Wikipedia languages) ├── vite-env.d.ts # Vite type declarations ├── contexts/ │ └── LikedArticlesContext.tsx # Like management + heart animation ├── hooks/ │ ├── useWikiArticles.ts # Wikipedia API fetching + buffering │ └── useLocalization.ts # Language persistence ├── components/ │ ├── WikiCard.tsx # Main card component (fullscreen card) │ ├── Article.tsx # Article display (semantic HTML) │ ├── ArticleList.tsx # Article list renderer │ └── LanguageSelector.tsx # Language dropdown ├── types/ │ └── ArticleProps.ts # ArticleProps interface ├── styles/ │ └── Article.css # Article container styles ├── assets/ │ └── heartAnimation.css # Heart animation keyframes └── stories/ # Storybook test stories ├── Button.tsx/stories.ts ├── Header.tsx/stories.ts ├── Page.tsx/stories.ts └── *.css files ``` --- ### 9. DEPENDENCIES SUMMARY **Core:** - React 18.3.1 + React-DOM 18.3.1 - Tailwind CSS 4.0.3 - Lucide React 0.474.0 (icons) - Vercel Analytics 1.4.1 **Build Tools:** - Vite 6.0.5 - TypeScript ~5.6.2 - Storybook (pre-release build) - Vitest + Playwright (testing) --- ### 10. REAL USAGE PATTERNS **Infinite Scroll Pattern:** - Uses Intersection Observer to detect when user scrolls near bottom - Triggers `fetchArticles()` with 100px margin - Articles buffered (20 pre-fetched) for smooth UX - Loading indicator shown during fetch **Like/Favorites Pattern:** - Double-click or heart button to like - Displays heart pop animation (0.5s ease-out from center-top) - Persists to localStorage immediately - Exportable as JSON with timestamp **Language Switching Pattern:** - Select language from dropdown - Saves to localStorage - Full page reload with new language variant - All API calls use `variant` parameter for correct title display **Share Pattern:** - Uses native Navigator.share() if available (mobile) - Falls back to clipboard copy on desktop - Alert confirmation on fallback --- ### 11. ANALYTICS & TRACKING - **Vercel Analytics** component rendered in App (no custom tracking) - No event tracking setup observed beyond page views --- All files have been thoroughly read and analyzed. The app is a TikTok-style Wikipedia browser ("WikiTok") with minimal complexity, no routing, single provider (likes context), and direct Wikipedia API integration across 40+ languages.

Now let me create a comprehensive summary report based on my thorough exploration: ## COMPREHENSIVE REACT APP CODEBASE ANALYSIS ### 1. PROVIDER TREE & CONTEXT **Full Provider Tree (Root → Leaf):** ``` createRoot(document.getElementById('root')!) ├── React.StrictMode │ └── LikedArticlesProvider │ └── App ``` **Provider Details:** - **LikedArticlesProvider**: `/src/contexts/LikedArticlesContext.tsx` - Manages liked articles state - Provides context: `{ likedArticles, toggleLike, isLiked }` - Renders heart animation overlay when article is liked - Custom hook: `useLikedArticles()` **External Library Providers:** - **Analytics**: `@vercel/analytics/react` (imported in App.tsx as `` component) --- ### 2. CSS FILES & IMPORTS **All CSS Files with Import Paths:** | File Path | Purpose | |-----------|---------| | `/src/index.css` | Global styles - Tailwind import, scrollbar hiding, body overflow handling | | `/src/assets/heartAnimation.css` | Heart pop animation for likes (imported in LikedArticlesContext) | | `/src/styles/Article.css` | Article container & content styling (imported via component use) | | `/src/stories/button.css` | Storybook button styles | | `/src/stories/header.css` | Storybook header styles | | `/src/stories/page.css` | Storybook page styles | **CSS Framework:** Tailwind CSS v4.0.3 (via `@tailwindcss/postcss` and `@tailwindcss/vite`) --- ### 3. DATA FETCHING QUERIES **All Fetch Calls:** **useWikiArticles Hook** (`/src/hooks/useWikiArticles.ts`): - **Function:** `fetchArticles(forBuffer?: boolean)` - **Method:** Native `fetch()` API - **Base URL Pattern:** `{currentLanguage.api}` (language-dependent Wikipedia endpoint) - **Query Parameters:** ``` action=query format=json generator=random grnnamespace=0 prop=extracts|info|pageimages inprop=url|varianttitles grnlimit=20 exintro=1 exlimit=max exsentences=5 explaintext=1 piprop=thumbnail pithumbsize=800 origin=* variant={currentLanguage.id} ``` - **Response Handling:** Parses Wikipedia query response, filters articles with thumbnails/extracts/URLs - **Image Preloading:** Uses `Promise.allSettled()` for image preload verification - **Buffer System:** Maintains 20-article buffer for smooth infinite scrolling **Wikipedia API Endpoints** (by language, from `/src/languages.ts`): - English: `https://en.wikipedia.org/w/api.php?` - Plus 40+ other language variants (Arabic, Bengali, Catalan, Czech, German, Spanish, French, Japanese, Chinese, etc.) --- ### 4. BROWSER STATE READS **localStorage Usage:** | Key | File | Purpose | |-----|------|---------| | `"lang"` | `/src/hooks/useLocalization.ts` | Persists selected language preference | | `"likedArticles"` | `/src/contexts/LikedArticlesContext.tsx` | Persists user's liked Wikipedia articles | **localStorage Operations:** 1. **Read on Init:** - `localStorage.getItem("lang")` - Line 6 (useLocalization.ts) - `localStorage.getItem("likedArticles")` - Line 16 (LikedArticlesContext.tsx) 2. **Write on Change:** - `localStorage.setItem("lang", currentLanguage.id)` - Line 15 (useLocalization.ts) - `localStorage.setItem("likedArticles", JSON.stringify(likedArticles))` - Line 23 (LikedArticlesContext.tsx) **No sessionStorage or cookies used.** --- ### 5. PORTALS & PORTAL ROOTS **Portal Root Elements:** - **Single Root:** `
` in `/index.html` (Line 15) - **Mount Point:** `createRoot(document.getElementById('root')!)` in `/src/main.tsx` (Line 7) **No React Portals (createPortal) used in this codebase.** All UI rendered as normal DOM hierarchy. --- ### 6. KEY COMPONENTS & PAGES **Component Tree & Props:** ``` App (Main Component) ├── WikiCard[] │ └── Props: { article: WikiArticle } │ └── Features: Image lazy loading, like/share actions, double-click to like ├── LanguageSelector │ └── Props: None (uses context) │ └── Features: Dropdown with 40+ language options ├── Analytics (from Vercel) │ └── Props: None └── Modal Overlays (inline, conditional render) ├── About Modal ├── Liked Articles Modal │ └── ArticleList (used here) │ └── Props: { articles: ArticleProps[], onArticleSelect? } │ └── Renders: Article components │ └── Article Component │ └── Props: { title, content, image? } ``` **Page Structure:** - **Single Page Application** - No routing (all components in App.tsx) - **Main UI:** Infinite-scrolling fullscreen cards (snap scroll) - **Modals:** 1. About modal (fixed overlay, z-index: 50) 2. Liked Articles modal with search functionality --- ### 7. ROUTING SETUP **Routing:** None. This is a **single-page React app** with no react-router or equivalent. Navigation: - Button clicks toggle local state (`showAbout`, `showLikes`) - Language changes trigger `window.location.reload()` - External links open in new tabs (Wikipedia articles) --- ### 8. PROJECT STRUCTURE SUMMARY ``` /src ├── main.tsx # Entry point, wraps App in providers ├── App.tsx # Main component (infinite scroll UI) ├── index.css # Global Tailwind styles ├── languages.ts # Language definitions (40+ Wikipedia languages) ├── vite-env.d.ts # Vite type declarations ├── contexts/ │ └── LikedArticlesContext.tsx # Like management + heart animation ├── hooks/ │ ├── useWikiArticles.ts # Wikipedia API fetching + buffering │ └── useLocalization.ts # Language persistence ├── components/ │ ├── WikiCard.tsx # Main card component (fullscreen card) │ ├── Article.tsx # Article display (semantic HTML) │ ├── ArticleList.tsx # Article list renderer │ └── LanguageSelector.tsx # Language dropdown ├── types/ │ └── ArticleProps.ts # ArticleProps interface ├── styles/ │ └── Article.css # Article container styles ├── assets/ │ └── heartAnimation.css # Heart animation keyframes └── stories/ # Storybook test stories ├── Button.tsx/stories.ts ├── Header.tsx/stories.ts ├── Page.tsx/stories.ts └── *.css files ``` --- ### 9. DEPENDENCIES SUMMARY **Core:** - React 18.3.1 + React-DOM 18.3.1 - Tailwind CSS 4.0.3 - Lucide React 0.474.0 (icons) - Vercel Analytics 1.4.1 **Build Tools:** - Vite 6.0.5 - TypeScript ~5.6.2 - Storybook (pre-release build) - Vitest + Playwright (testing) --- ### 10. REAL USAGE PATTERNS **Infinite Scroll Pattern:** - Uses Intersection Observer to detect when user scrolls near bottom - Triggers `fetchArticles()` with 100px margin - Articles buffered (20 pre-fetched) for smooth UX - Loading indicator shown during fetch **Like/Favorites Pattern:** - Double-click or heart button to like - Displays heart pop animation (0.5s ease-out from center-top) - Persists to localStorage immediately - Exportable as JSON with timestamp **Language Switching Pattern:** - Select language from dropdown - Saves to localStorage - Full page reload with new language variant - All API calls use `variant` parameter for correct title display **Share Pattern:** - Uses native Navigator.share() if available (mobile) - Falls back to clipboard copy on desktop - Alert confirmation on fallback --- ### 11. ANALYTICS & TRACKING - **Vercel Analytics** component rendered in App (no custom tracking) - No event tracking setup observed beyond page views --- All files have been thoroughly read and analyzed. The app is a TikTok-style Wikipedia browser ("WikiTok") with minimal complexity, no routing, single provider (likes context), and direct Wikipedia API integration across 40+ languages.

Clean. Here's a summary of what was set up:

**`.storybook/preview.tsx`** — Updated with:
- `LikedArticlesProvider` global decorator (matches real app's provider tree)
- `../src/index.css` import (Tailwind + global styles)
- MSW initialized with `onUnhandledRequest: 'bypass'`
- `beforeEach` seeds `localStorage` for `lang` and `likedArticles` (both read on init)

**`.storybook/main.ts`** — Added `msw-storybook-addon` and `staticDirs: ['../public']` for the MSW service worker

**`.storybook/msw-handlers.ts`** — Wikipedia API handler matching all `*.wikipedia.org/w/api.php` URLs, returning 3 deterministic mock articles

**5 story files, 16 stories total:**

| File | Stories |
|------|---------|
| `Article.stories.tsx` | Default, WithImage, LongContent |
| `ArticleList.stories.tsx` | Default, SingleArticle, WithOnSelect |
| `WikiCard.stories.tsx` | Default, Liked (pre-seeded localStorage), LikeInteraction (click to like) |
| `LanguageSelector.stories.tsx` | Default, DropdownOpen, LanguageSearch |
| `App.stories.tsx` | Default (MSW-backed articles), AboutModalOpen, LikesModalOpen, EmptyLikesModal |

Every story has a `play` function with meaningful assertions verifying provider-backed state, MSW mock data, localStorage seeding, and interaction outcomes.
